title: USDC Layer Format Open Contract

included: .usdc resolved-extension fast-path participation for usd-layer-open dispatch
opened resource byte handoff to usdc-spec-parser
canonical layer dump adapter pass-through only at validation boundaries
deferred: resource path classification and file reads, owned by usd-resource-protocol and usd-layer-open

model.LayerFormatHandler: Concrete handler registered with usd-layer-open for .usdc resources.
model.OpenedResourceBytes: Bytes or a file-backed byte view supplied by usd-layer-open after resource opening. This is not a USD payload composition arc.
model.LayerOpenResult: A target-native result containing either a document-model Layer or diagnostics. It is not a JSON object.
model.CanonicalDump: The layer-dump JSON shape used only by adapters and integration goldens.

output.matches: boolean candidate decision for .usdc layer resources

input.ok: precondition: ResourceReadResult.ok is true; failed reads are diagnosed by usd-layer-open before handler invocation
input.bytes: opened resource bytes supplied by usd-layer-open
input.file_backed_path: optional filesystem path from usd-resource-protocol when the resource is LocalFile-backed

output.result: target-native LayerOpenResult containing a document-model Layer or diagnostics

laws: .usdc resources are USDC layer candidates and may be selected by usd-layer-open before content is read for PXR-USDC magic inspection
.usdz resources are not accepted by this handler
resource I/O and unsupported resource diagnostics are not owned by this handler
open-failure is reported by usd-layer-open before bytes reach this handler; the handler's ResourceReadResult input is successful by contract
when selected by resolved extension, the handler validates opened bytes through usdc-binary-format and must not redispatch based on opened byte content
non-PXR-USDC opened bytes fail with diagnostics before partial layer output
non-PXR-USDC and unsupported-version diagnostics must be produced from usdc-binary-format outputs, not from local string scans of the opened resource bytes
valid USDC bytes are parsed through usdc-spec-parser
open_usdc_layer must not return Json, dict, tagged JSON, or canonical dump trees as its domain result
opened resource bytes must not be described as payloads in this contract; payload is reserved for USD payload composition arcs and Crate value-representation payload fields
implementations should avoid copying the entire opened resource when a safe byte span, string view, memory map, or equivalent view can be handed to the parser
JSON materialization is allowed only in explicit adapters, diagnostics serialization, and dump commands
canonical dump output must be obtained through an explicit adapter; returning canned dump JSON selected by file content or section names is a contract violation

the handler must accept opened resource bytes from usd-layer-open; invoking usd-resource-protocol reads (read_resource, read_binary_file, or equivalent) directly with a raw identifier string is a contract violation

when file_backed_path is present on the opened resource, the handler must forward it to usdc-binary-format so the Crate decoder can elect a memory-mapped fast path; copying the opened bytes into an owned vector before decoding is a contract violation when a file-backed path is available
this contract must not call USDA lexer, USDA value parser, or USDA spec parser

adapter_protocol.input: Unit adapters may accept a JSON case with opened resource bytes supplied as fixture paths or hex strings.
adapter_protocol.output: Adapters convert LayerOpenResult to the ok, diagnostics, and layer JSON shape in contracts/layer-dump.schema.json.
